    Seth Watts

    Assistant Professor of Criminal Justice at Texas State University. I study policing, technology, and public health.

    • San Marcos, TX
    • Texas State University

    About

    I examine how emerging tools — from AI-assisted body-worn cameras to co-response models — reshape police behavior, officer decision-making, and community outcomes. My work sits at the intersection of criminology, public health, and applied policy evaluation.

    Assistant Professor, School of Criminal Justice and Criminology, Texas State University · 2025 – present

    Research Affiliate, Texas Crime & Justice Center, Texas State University · 2025 – present

    PhD, Criminology & Criminal Justice, Arizona State University, 2024

    MS, Criminology & Criminal Justice, Arizona State University, 2021

    BA, Psychology, Bowling Green State University, 2019
